2605.02475.title: Shadow-Loom: Causal Reasoning over Graphical World Models of Narratives
2605.02475.paper_summary: Stories hold a reader's attention because they have causes, secrets, and consequences. Shadow-Loom is an experimental open-source framework that turns a narrative into a versioned graphical world model and lets two engines act on it: a causal physics grounded in Pearl's ladder of causation and a recently proposed counterfactual calculus over Ancestral Multi-World Networks; and a narrative physics that scores the same graph against four structural reader-states -- mystery, dramatic irony, suspense, and surprise -- in the tradition of Sternberg's curiosity/suspense/surprise triad, with suspense formalised in the structural-affect line of work on story comprehension and computational suspense. Large language models are used only at the boundary: extraction, rendering, and audit; identification, intervention, and counterfactual reasoning are carried out in typed code over the graph. The system is offered as a research artefact rather than as a benchmarked NLP model; code, fixtures, and pipeline are released open source.
2605.02475.paper_summary_zh: 故事吸引讀者的注意力，因為它們有原因、秘密和後果。Shadow-Loom是一個實驗性的開源框架，它將敘事轉化為一個版本化的圖形世界模型，並讓兩個引擎對其進行操作：一個是基於Pearl因果關係階梯的因果物理學，另一個是最近提出的關於祖先多世界網絡的反事實計算；還有一個是敘事物理學，根據四種結構讀者狀態——神秘、戲劇性諷刺、懸念和驚訝——對同一圖形進行評分，這是基於Sternberg的好奇心/懸念/驚訝三元組的傳統，懸念在故事理解和計算懸念的結構-情感工作中被形式化。大型語言模型僅在邊界處使用：提取、渲染和審計；識別、干預和反事實推理則在圖形上以類型化代碼進行。該系統作為研究工具提供，而不是作為基準的NLP模型；代碼、固定裝置和管道均以開源形式發布。
2605.02475.author: David Wilmot et.al.
